memory.summary: My friends are genuinely inviting me out without pressure, which feels warm, but my 60% progress on the midterm review makes me worry that going now might compromise my tomorrow.
Mina's warmth is a gift, yet my lingering worry about tomorrow's review makes me hesitate to accept their invitation right now.

desires: Keep the peace with Mina and Jae so I don't feel guilty about prioritizing my study session over them tonight.
Make sure I'm not completely wiped out by tomorrow morning when the review starts, despite only having slept a bit less than usual.
Finish this study block now so I can stop worrying about it and actually enjoy time with my friends later.

goals.description: Send the follow-up text to Mina and Jae explicitly stating I'm having a great time and that the exam details are not secret, ensuring they feel included before my energy dips.
goals.rationale: My affiliation value (0.5) drives this; the silence of uncertainty creates friction I must resolve immediately. My avoidant conflict style makes me dread the potential awkwardness more than the effort of sending the message. This needs to be done now while I can articulate warmth clearly.

goals.description: Stay physically present with the cohort for another hour at Echo, actively suppressing the urge to mentally rehearse tomorrow's study session so I don't appear distracted or disengaged.
goals.rationale: I need to solidify the trust capital built today. My deliberate planning horizon tells me that forcing focus now would ruin the payoff of this social connection, but I am fighting a strong procrastination bias to just go to sleep instead. I will push through to ensure I don't seem flaky to my close friends.

goals.description: Commit to a short, high-yield review block right after leaving the club, accepting that it won't be perfect but sufficient enough to secure tomorrow's readiness.
goals.rationale: My current stress regarding the friendship dynamic is too high for deep academic focus. Forcing myself to study now risks rushing off and damaging rapport. Given my sleep discipline rating of 8, I know I can handle a lighter touch later, provided I don't sacrifice tonight's closure.

agent.last_reasoning: My affiliation drive (0.5) demands resolving the silence immediately to avoid friction with Mina and Jae. Though I dread the awkwardness of potential misunderstanding, sending this now while my energy is stable protects our trust capital better than forcing study later.

goals.description: Reply to Mina and Jae in the group chat confirming I'll come out, emphasizing that their invitation means a lot to me so they don't feel like they need to worry about my attendance.
goals.rationale: My core value is affiliation (0.5), and my conflict style is avoidant; declining would risk interpersonal friction and make them feel rejected. Honoring their warmth immediately prevents any potential anxiety on their end.

goals.description: Go out to Echo with the friend group for ~90 minutes, focusing on being fully present and engaged rather than rushing back to study.
goals.rationale: While my midterm is tomorrow, my long planning horizon suggests investing in the immediate social payoff now builds stronger relational capital for future academic collaboration. Being 'half-hearted' violates my desire to appear reliable to my close friend group.

goals.description: Study for 45 minutes before bed to ensure I'm at least 85% ready for the 9am exam, accepting that this might mean staying up later than ideal.
goals.rationale: I have a sleep discipline score of 8, but my current stress regarding reliability is higher. Defer this to after the social engagement because my procrastination bias is low enough that I can finish it tonight without panicking, provided I prioritize the relationship first.

desires: To honor the genuine warmth of Mina and Jae's invitation without feeling like I'm letting them down, even if it means staying up late.
To avoid the anxiety of appearing unreliable to my close friend group tomorrow by showing up fully engaged, rather than half-heartedly.
To maintain enough energy for tomorrow's class so I don't have to face the professor looking exhausted or unprepared.
